title: Forces and Newton's Laws of Motion
description: Newton's three laws of motion, force as a vector, weight and normal reaction, F = ma for particles and simple systems, and combining forces to analyse equilibrium.
objectives: State Newton's first, second and third laws and apply them to everyday situations
Distinguish mass (a scalar, in kg) from weight (a force, W = mg, in newtons)
Model normal reaction forces and use free-body diagrams for particles on horizontal and inclined surfaces
Apply F = ma to single particles and simple connected systems such as a lift or a particle on a slope
Combine forces acting on a particle as vectors to find a resultant, and resolve a force into perpendicular components
Recognise and use equilibrium (resultant force = 0) to solve for unknown forces

lessons.title: Weight, normal reaction and F = ma
